quality.rationale: 结合爱因斯坦静态宇宙度规与物质密度求尺度因子，进而通过积分计算全空间体积，物理逻辑清晰且具经典背景。

origin.question: Problem 19.32. What is the proper volume of the "Einstein universe" of Problem 19.31, in terms of $\\rho_0$ , the density of its dust.
origin.solution:
Solution 19.32. The line element of the "Einstein universe" $(\mathbf{k} = +1)$ is (see Problem 19.5)
$$
\mathrm {d s}^{2} = \mathbf {R}^{2} \left[ \mathrm {d} \chi^{2} + \sin^{2} \chi (\mathrm {d} \theta^{2} + \sin^{2} \theta \mathrm {d} \phi^{2}) \right] \tag {1}
$$
with
$$
0 \leq \chi \leq \pi , \quad 0 \leq \theta \leq \pi , \quad 0 \leq \phi \leq 2 \pi .
$$
The volume V is therefore
$$
V = \int_{0}^{\pi} 4 \pi R^{3} \sin^{2} \chi d \chi = 2 \pi^{2} R^{3}. \tag {2}
$$
From Problem 19.31, we have the result, for this universe, that
$$
R = \left(4 \pi \rho_{0}\right)^{- \frac {1}{2}} \tag {3}
$$
where $ho_0$ is the matter (dust) energy density. Substitution of Equation (3) into Equation (2) yields
$$
V = 2 \pi^{2} \left(\frac {c^{2}}{4 \pi G \rho_{0}}\right)^{\frac {3}{2}} \tag {4}
$$
(where we have put in the factors of c and G).
